name: Olive Exclusive Boutique Hotel
amenities: 7 themed suites named after Namibian regions

content.verdict: Olive Exclusive is the obvious gateway hotel for a Namibia honeymoon — a polished, only-seven-suite boutique tucked into the leafy Klein Windhoek suburb, 20 minutes from Hosea Kutako International Airport and a quick drive to the bush plane charter terminal. Each suite is named after and decorated in the spirit of a Namibian region (Caprivi, Damaraland, Kalahari, Kavango, Kunene, Skeleton Coast, Sossusvlei) with art and textiles from that area; the Sossusvlei Suite, for example, is washed in apricot and rust tones with photography of the dunes, while the Caprivi Suite leans green and tropical. All seven have a private terrace, walk-in shower, and full-size tub. The hotel is built around a low-slung pool deck shaded by olive trees, with a small spa treatment room, a serious art collection, and a 16-cover restaurant that is consistently among Windhoek's best dinners. As a one-or-two-night arrival or departure stop, it sets the tone for the trip beautifully — couples land jet-lagged, are met by a driver, eat a real meal, sleep in a quiet design suite, and wake up the next morning ready for the bush plane. It does not pretend to be a destination property: there is no reason to stay more than two nights, but it is by some distance the best place to start and finish a Namibia trip.
content.best_room: The Sossusvlei Suite (the largest and brightest, with the longest pool view) and the Skeleton Coast Suite (the most private, with a separate entrance) are the two honeymoon picks. The Damaraland Suite has the largest bathroom with a freestanding tub.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival in Windhoek
itinerary_7_nights.description: Land at Hosea Kutako International, met by Olive Exclusive's driver. 20-minute transfer to Klein Windhoek, check in to your themed suite, light lunch, slow afternoon by the pool. Welcome dinner at the Olive Restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Onward to Sossusvlei
itinerary_7_nights.description: Light breakfast in the garden, transfer to Eros Airport for the bush flight to Sossusvlei (about 1 hour). Beginning of the safari portion of the trip.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sossusvlei desert (off-property)
itinerary_7_nights.description: On honeymoon at Little Kulala or Sossusvlei Desert Lodge — see those itineraries for detail.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Damaraland or Skeleton Coast (off-property)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Continue the loop by light aircraft into northern Namibia.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Etosha (off-property)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final safari stop — Onguma The Fort or another Etosha-border lodge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Return to Windhoek
itinerary_7_nights.description: Light-aircraft transfer back to Eros Airport, driver to Olive Exclusive. Quiet evening, in-room spa, last dinner at the Olive Restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast, driver transfer to Hosea Kutako International for the international flight home.

caveats: Windhoek is a city, not a destination. Use Olive Exclusive as a one-night arrival and one-night departure stop, not as a multi-day base.
Hosea Kutako International Airport is 45 minutes away (not 20) on quiet roads — check transfer timing carefully on departure day.
Eros Airport, where most bush flights depart, is in central Windhoek 15 minutes from the hotel — confirm which airport your bush flight uses.
Pool is unheated and Windhoek nights can be cool from May to August.
Restaurant has limited covers and books up most evenings; reserve on check-in.

faqs.question: Why stay here rather than at a chain hotel near the airport?
faqs.answer: After a long international flight, the difference between an airport business hotel and a quiet seven-suite boutique with a real restaurant and a small pool is enormous — and it sets the right tone for the honeymoon. Olive Exclusive is also closer to Eros Airport for the onward bush flight than the airport hotels.

faqs.question: Is the food really good?
faqs.answer: Yes — the Olive Restaurant is consistently rated one of the best in Namibia. Tasting menu with local game and Namibian wines is the recommended honeymoon dinner.

faqs.question: How does the airport transfer work?
faqs.answer: The hotel arranges complimentary transfer on request — confirm at booking. The drive from Hosea Kutako International to the hotel is about 45 minutes through quiet semi-desert and is a gentle introduction to Namibia.

faqs.question: Can we book all our safari logistics through the hotel?
faqs.answer: The concierge can confirm and coordinate transfers, but most couples arrive with their safari already booked through a specialist operator. Olive Exclusive's role is the gateway, not the planner.

faqs.question: How many nights should we stay here?
faqs.answer: One night on arrival is essential; one night on departure is highly recommended. More than two nights and the city limits start to show — Windhoek is pleasant but small.

faqs.question: Is it really a honeymoon hotel?
faqs.answer: It is more a sophisticated boutique stop than a honeymoon destination property in itself, but for a couple beginning or ending a Namibia honeymoon trip, it is the most romantic and best-run city hotel in the country.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Olive Exclusive Boutique Hotel, [arrival date]

Dear Reservations,

We are arriving in Windhoek on [date] for our Namibia honeymoon and would like:

- One night on arrival in the Sossusvlei or Skeleton Coast Suite
- One night on departure on [return date] — same suite if possible
- Complimentary airport transfer from Hosea Kutako International on arrival, and to Hosea Kutako on departure
- Dinner reservation at the Olive Restaurant on both nights
- Any honeymoon arrangements (welcome champagne, in-room spa)

Thank you very much.

Kind regards,
[Names]
